questions.title: COPD Management
questions.text: A patient with a history of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) presents with increased shortness of breath and wheezing. Which intervention should be prioritized?
questions.rationale: Administering a bronchodilator is crucial for relieving bronchospasm and improving airflow in COPD exacerbations. While other interventions may be helpful, bronchodilation is the priority to improve respiratory function.

questions.title: Pain Assessment
questions.text: A post-operative patient reports pain of 8/10. Which interventions should the nurse implement? Select all that apply.

choices.text: Wait for the next scheduled assessment

questions.rationale: Proper pain management requires comprehensive assessment, documentation, medication administration, and non-pharmacological interventions. Waiting for the next scheduled assessment would delay necessary pain relief.

questions.title: IV Flow Rate
questions.text: If a patient needs to receive 1000mL of IV fluid over 8 hours, what should the drip rate be in drops per minute? (Assume drop factor is 15 gtts/mL)
